data.title: TikTok Owner ByteDance in $7B Talks to Sell Mobile Legends Gaming Unit to Saudi Arabia
data.description:
TikTok owner ByteDance is reportedly getting set to sell its gaming unit Moonton to a Saudi Arabian peer for around $7 billion.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Mobile Legends on Move

According to a Reuters report, ByteDance is in advanced talks to sell Shanghai Moonton Technology, the studio behind the popular mobile game Mobile Legends, to Savvy Games Group for between $6 billion and $7 billion.

According to Reuters sources, a deal could be done as soon as this quarter. It is understood that the companies have already reached an initial agreement on the broad terms of the deal.

ByteDance's video games unit Nuverse bought Moonton Technology back in 2021 in a deal believed to be worth around $4 billion. It is understood that Chinese video games and social media company Tencent (TCEHY) also made a bid for Moonton at the time.

Mobile Legends: Bang Bang is one of the most popular MOBA (Multiplayer Online Battle Arena) mobile games in the world. It won "Most Competitive Game of the Year" at the MOBIES 2024 and "Esports Mobile Game of the Year" at the Esports Awards 2024.

Other Moonton games include Magic Chess: Go Go and the gothic vampire game Silver and Blood.

Demand Keeps Growing

Savvy's games include Monopoly Go!, which has had over 150 million downloads around the world. It is an investor in Hero Esports, formerly VSPO, the largest esports company in Asia and it sponsors Saudi football club Al-Hilal.

The ​Moonton sale ​would add to an already busy ‌dealmaking activity in the ​videogame industry, ​as companies race to lock up durable franchises, live-service expertise and large online ​communities.

The global mobile gaming market size was estimated at $139.38 billion in 2024 and is projected to reach $256.19 billion by 2030 as smartphone adoption increases around the world.

We have rounded up the best Chinese stocks to buy now using our TipRanks comparison tool.
data.author: David Craik

data.title: Applied Materials Stock (AMAT) Soars as AI Demand Poised to Fuel 2026 Growth
data.description:
Shares in Applied Materials (AMAT) jumped about 12% early Friday after the chipmaking equipment maker beat Wall Street expectations for its first-quarter 2026 results and received a series of price target upgrades from analysts. CEO Gary Dickerson also expects robust artificial intelligence demand and ongoing memory chip supply shortages to drive a further 20% growth by the end of the fiscal year.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During the quarter that ended on January 25, the California-based company posted earnings per share of $2.54 on revenue of $7.01 billion. While EPS came in flat from the year-ago quarter and revenue dropped 2% year-over-year, both metrics beat analysts' consensus of $2.21 and revenue of $6.88 billion.

Applied Materials Achieves Record DRAM Revenue

Applied Materials also improved its gross margin to 49%, up from 48.8% a year ago. Dickerson attributed the "strong results" to accelerated investment in AI computing by Big Tech companies. In particular, the chief executive noted that the race for more energy-efficient AI chips drove demand for leading-edge logic, that is, equipment to manufacture the world's most advanced processors, such as the 2-nanometer processors.

The trend also led to more demand for equipment needed to produce high-bandwidth memory chips, whose prices have been soaring due to heavy spending on data centers by major U.S. tech firms. In addition, there is higher demand for advanced packing, which is the equipment and materials used to connect multiple chips or chiplets into a single, high-performance module.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: TheStreet Pro: Why Most Traders Fail and How You Can Become Part of the Top 5% Who Succeed
data.description:
It's true. Most traders fail. It's what the statistics say, and from my time on Wall Street as a Market Maker during the dotcom bust, I've seen it firsthand. Does that mean that trading is a fool's errand? Absolutely not.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Smart traders, who approach trading like the very competitive business that it is, can earn a substantial living. TheStreet Pro's James "Rev Shark" DePorre is one of those guys. He started trading decades ago and has dodged the statistics ever since.

Last Saturday, he published his guide on how you can become part of the successful 5% of traders. I'll summarize it below, or you can read the original here on TheStreet Pro in:  "Why 95% of Traders Fail – And the Strategy Used by Those Who Consistently Win."

Why do Most Traders Fail?

Some people think it's a conspiracy. That some unknown "they" lurking in the shadows are taking advantage of the little guy. That couldn't be farther from the truth. In reality, the issue is that most traders simply don't approach trading like a business. They take random shots in the dark, without really analyzing what works.

Rev Shark has the answer. We need to use effective, repeatable strategies and not overtrade.

Overtrading is the big killer of trading accounts. Rev Shark tells us that the failure rate for pure day traders is much higher than for short-term swing traders. Why? Day traders are more likely to overtrade, treating the market like a video game. They trade volatility, which, at least intraday, can be random.

Day traders also need a high win rate on their trades to overcome the inevitable bad trades. Swing traders have greater control over their win:loss ratio and can handle a lower win rate because those wins tend to be big enough to outweigh smaller losses.

Learn To Do Nothing

It sounds counterintuitive, but you're getting paid to watch the market and analyze it, looking for that "fat pitch" to swing at. What's a "fat pitch?" It's when technical setups, fundamental catalysts, and market sentiment all align. Your broker gets paid when you trade. You only get paid when you're right.

Rev Shark says that patience is a position. Cash is a holding, and you should only take those trades that will pay you more than cash. You should also focus on the quality of your trades. It's better to watch a few good opportunities than many mediocre ones. And you should trade like a sniper. Snipers spend 99% of their time watching and studying, and just 1% taking action.

Three Rules for Becoming a More Patient Trader

If you want to be part of the profitable 5%, here are three rules from Rev Shark:

  1. Wait for the setup: If the market is in a sloppy, trendless range, do nothing.
  2. Size up on conviction: When the stars align – like a biotechnology breakout with a clearly defined catalyst – that is when you stop being patient and start being aggressive.
  3. Pay yourself: Maybe you're in it for the love of trading and would do it for free. But that growing trading account doesn't do you any good unless you enjoy the fruits of your labor. Rev Shark, for example, pays his bills out of a small account that he trades aggressively.

Rev Shark emphasizes that doing less will make you more money over time. But remember that he's talking about how much trading you do. Overtrading is bad. Rather, you should treat trading like the serious and difficult business that it is. The main part of your job is research. Trading is just the part of the job where you implement your plan.

Membership in TheStreet Pro has its Privileges

In addition to strategy articles like this one, every day on TheStreet Pro, we publish trading ideas and market commentary to help you find the best trades. Plus, you have access to a community that includes Rev Shark, Doug Kass, and others. The best part is that today is a great time to become a member of TheStreet Pro. An annual subscription is currently half off during our President's Day sale. You can learn more here.

I hope you enjoyed this article and that Rev Shark's advice will make you a more profitable trader.
data.author: Jason Meshnick, The Street Pro

data.title: The Market's Biggest Test Yet? Nvidia (NVDA) Heads Into Earnings
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA) will report its fourth-quarter fiscal 2026 results on February 25, and investors are watching closely for what the company says about demand for AI chips. After a year in which its processors helped lift much of the market, traders want to know whether large cloud companies are still spending heavily on AI data centers. The company's comments could affect not only Nvidia's stock but also the mood across the broader tech sector.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Nvidia is the leading supplier of chips used to train and run AI systems. Because of this role, investors often treat its guidance as a signal for broader AI spending, not just a company-specific update.

What Investors Are Watching

Over the past year, Nvidia's results have repeatedly exceeded forecasts, helped by tight supply of AI chips and aggressive spending by large cloud providers.

The upcoming report matters because comparisons are becoming harder and investors want to know whether that pace of growth can continue.

In particular, attention will center on the rollout of Nvidia's next-generation Blackwell platform. Analysts are watching shipment timing, production capacity, and customer demand, as these factors will influence revenue growth over the coming quarters.

Wall Street Expectations from Nvidia

Wall Street expects another strong report, with earnings and revenue projected to rise sharply from a year ago. Data-center products, which include the company's AI accelerators, continue to be the main growth driver.

For Q4, analysts expect Nvidia to post adjusted earnings per share (EPS) of $1.52, up nearly 71% from the year-ago figure of $0.89. Moreover, sales are forecast to jump 67% year-over-year to $65.56 billion.

What the Market Wants to Hear

For this report, investors will pay close attention to management's outlook. They will listen for updates on orders from large cloud customers, spending plans, and whether companies building AI systems are still expanding capacity.

If Nvidia indicates that demand remains strong, it would support the view that the AI build-out is still in its early stages. However, any signs of slower orders or delayed deployments could raise concerns that expectations have moved ahead of reality.

As a result, the report is being watched beyond the company itself. Many investors now see Nvidia as a gauge for AI spending, so its comments could influence sentiment across the wider technology sector.

Is Nvidia a Good Stock to Buy?

On TipRanks, Nvidia stock commands a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 37 Buys, one Hold, and one Sell rating. The average Nvidia price target of $260.38 implies 39.29% upside potential from current levels.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Tesla Stock: Energy Boom vs. EV Bust – Can FSD Drive 2026 Recovery?
data.description:
Shares of electric vehicle (EV) giant Tesla (TSLA) traded around $411-$420 in early 2026, down 7% year-to-date amid EV sales declines. Yet, Tesla's most bullish analyst eyes potential 40%+ upside from energy storage growth and Full Self-Driving (FSD) monetization.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

EV Challenges Weigh Heavy

Tesla's Q4 FY25 deliveries dropped 15% year-over-year to 418,000 units, marking a second annual decline. Fierce competition from Chinese rival BYD (BYDDF) and softening demand in Europe, where registrations fell over 40%, have led to the declines. Leadership shake-ups, including a new global sales head, highlight internal efforts to counter eroding market share and margin-squeezing price wars.

Energy Business Powers Ahead

Energy storage deployments hit records in 2025, positioning Megapack and Powerwall for $12 to $15 billion in revenue by 2027, diversifying beyond vehicles hit by macroeconomic headwinds. This high-margin segment could offset auto weakness, with 2025 revenue at $95 billion despite a 2.9% dip. Investors see it as a stabilizing force amid Tesla's pivot to software-led growth.

FSD and Robotics to the Rescue

Tesla's FSD subscriptions could generate $50-$100 monthly per vehicle, potentially lifting margins to 13%-20% by 2028 if regulatory approvals accelerate. Tesla's $20 billion 2026 capex guidance targets autonomous driving and Optimus robots. CEO Elon Musk plans to end production of premium Model S and Model X at its California factories to reallocate capacity to Optimus.

Analysts' Views on Tesla Stock

Tigress Financial analyst Ivan Feinseth initiated coverage with a Buy rating and $550 price target, implying 31.9% upside potential. He sees Tesla shifting its focus from a pure-play EV maker to a broad AI platform featuring self-driving tech. He noted that growth will come from more FSD subscriptions, robotaxis, and Optimus robots, adding to its strong EV and energy businesses.

Morgan Stanley analyst Andrew Percoco maintained his Hold rating and $415 price target, suggesting that shares are fully valued at current levels. He sees Tesla's solar manufacturing push as a smart long-term bet on geopolitics and data center demand. At full capacity, Tesla Solar could add $20 billion to $50 billion in value ($6 to $14 per share) to its $140 billion energy business ($40 per share). Although not huge on a standalone basis, vertical integration could boost growth.

Is Tesla Stock a Good Buy Right Now?

Analysts remain cautious about Tesla's long-term prospects. On TipRanks, TSLA stock has a Hold consensus rating based on 12 Buys, 11 Holds, and seven Sell ratings. The average Tesla price target of $396.80 implies 4.9% downside potential from current levels. Over the past year, TSLA shares have gained 17%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 2/13/26 – U.S. Stock Futures Wobble Ahead of Key CPI Data
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were mixed early Friday as traders anticipate the key consumer price index (CPI) data this morning. Economists forecast inflation rising 2.5% year-over-year and 0.3% from the previous month. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) and the S&P 500 (SPX) were up 0.13% and 0.02%, respectively, while those on the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.13% at 6:10 a.m. EST on February 13.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During Thursday's regular trading, all three indexes closed lower amid growing fears that AI is slowly taking over other sectors such as real estate, trucking, and software, after hitting software and finance stocks earlier in the week. The S&P 500 fell 1.6%, the Nasdaq Composite dropped 2%, and the DJIA slipped 1.3%.

After markets closed, Applied Materials (AMAT) jumped 11% after reporting strong earnings and guidance. Airbnb (ABNB) gained around 2% on upbeat outlook comments, while Pinterest (PINS) slid 14% following weak quarterly results and a soft forecast. Moreover, Apple (AAPL) dropped 5%, recording its biggest one-day decline since April 2025, on news that it may delay its planned AI Siri upgrade.

On the earnings front, notable companies including Moderna (MRNA) and Wendy's (WEN) will report earnings today.

Notably, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was down, floating near 4.12%. WTI crude oil futures were trending higher, hovering near $62.96 per barrel as of the last check. Additionally, the Gold Spot U.S. dollar price decreased to nearly $4,973 per ounce on Friday.

Elsewhere, European indexes opened mixed today amid a flurry of mixed corporate earnings.

Asia-Pacific Markets Traded Lower

Asia-Pacific markets traded lower on February 13, tracking Wall Street losses.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index fell 1.72%. In China, the Shanghai Composite dropped 1.26%, while the Shenzhen Component lost 1.41%. Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei fell 1.21%, and the Topix decreased 1.63%.

data.title: Xiaomi YU7 Beats Tesla Model Y in China with Over 37.8K January Sales
data.description:
Chinese company Xiaomi (XIACF) (HK:1810) is giving Tesla (TSLA) a tough time, with its electric vehicles (EVs) that are seeing robust demand in the domestic market. Notably, the company's YU7 SUV topped the charts in China based on January sales and sold 37,869 units, twice that of Tesla's Model Y's 16,845 deliveries. The numbers are based on data from the China Passenger Car Association (CPCA).

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Xiaomi Amps Up Rivalry with Tesla

Xiaomi YU7's 37,869 sales in January marked 70.3% year-over-year growth, though they declined 22.3% sequentially. Interestingly, Tesla's Model Y was the top-selling model in December 2025, but dropped to 20th place in January 2026 (numbers include both electric and gasoline-powered vehicles).

The Chinese EV maker launched YU7, its second EV model, in June 2025 and positioned it as a competitor to Tesla's Model Y. Xiaomi offered its YU7 at a lower price than Model Y in China and boasted that it is better than Tesla's EV on key features, including driving range on a single battery charge.

The robust January sales data for Xiaomi's YU7 came despite concerns about a slowdown in the Chinese EV market due to macro uncertainty and intense rivalry. Xiaomi founder, chairman, and CEO Lei Jun highlighted that the YU7 was the best-selling model in China by retail sales in January, citing auto media outlet Auto Home.

Meanwhile, sales of Xiaomi's SU7 electric sedan series continued to decline as the company gears up to launch an updated model. On February 10, Lei announced that the company had officially ended the production of the first-generation of SU7. Overall, Xiaomi, which was already popular for its smartphones, sold 39,002 EVs in January 2026 and is rapidly establishing its position in the EV market. After selling 411,837 EVs in 2025, Xiaomi is targeting 550,000 vehicles in 2026.

Wall Street's Take on Xiaomi and Tesla Stocks

According to TipRanks' Stock Comparison Tool, Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on Xiaomi's U.S.-listed shares, with the average XIACF stock price target of $6.76 indicating about 47% upside potential. Meanwhile, Wall Street is sidelined on Tesla stock, with the average price target implying 5% downside risk.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Why Are Stock Market Futures Up Today, 2/12/26? Inflation Report Ahead
data.description:
U.S. stock futures traded higher late Thursday night after a weak day on Wall Street, while traders waited for a key inflation report due Friday morning. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 (SPX) Index were up 0.23%, 0.09%, and 0.16%, respectively, at 9:48 p.m. EDT on February 12.

In Thursday's session, the major indexes fell as worries about artificial-intelligence disruption spread to sectors such as real estate, trucking, and software. The S&P 500 dropped about 1.6%, the Nasdaq Composite fell roughly 2%, and the Dow Jones Industrial Average lost nearly 670 points, or 1.3%.

In after-hours trading, Applied Materials (AMAT) jumped 11% after strong earnings and guidance. Airbnb (ABNB) gained around 2% on upbeat outlook comments, while Pinterest (PINS) slid 14% following weak quarterly results and a soft forecast.

All of the "Magnificent Seven" technology stocks finished lower. Cisco Systems (CSCO) fell 12% after disappointing guidance, and Apple (AAPL) dropped 5%, its biggest one-day decline since April 2025.

Investors are now focused on Friday's consumer price index report. Economists expect inflation to rise 2.5% from a year ago and 0.3% from the prior month.
